title: Profiling, Event-Loop Health, Memory, Load, and Capacity · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed operations handoff reconstructs profiling, event-loop health, memory, load, and capacity from bounded code and request evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the performance experiment and capacity envelope. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for performance experiment and capacity envelope after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate user latency and throughput outcomes into request-path budgets, resource hypotheses, experiment controls, and stop conditions before optimizing.
Use CPU profiles, heap snapshots, allocation, garbage collection, event-loop delay, handles, and request traces to locate causal bottlenecks safely.
Model arrival rate, concurrency, payload, endpoint mix, warmup, caches, connection reuse, percentiles, coordinated omission, errors, and saturation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Performance Hypothesis Budget
steps.instruction:
Order the Performance Hypothesis Budget Node and Express server HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Performance Hypothesis Budget.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Performance Hypothesis Budget assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Node and Express server Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Performance Hypothesis Budget, keep Performance Hypothesis Budget connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A review team handles case 8170 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate tail latency with event-loop and utilization capacity evidence; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate user latency and throughput outcomes into request-path budgets, resource hypotheses, experiment controls, and stop conditions before optimizing.

content.text: Order work so Performance Hypothesis Budget has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
